target.name: Kairos Power
target.founder: Mike Laufer, Edward Blandford, Per Peterson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: TerraPower
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $4B+

data.narrative:
Kairos Power is a late-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $500M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.6B. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 firms in the nuclear energy sector—three public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and seven private companies including TerraPower, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Zap Energy.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $1.6B last post-money valuation, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables—TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Nano Nuclear Energy ($1.5B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B)—produces a median of $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.0B.

Synthesizing both methods, the analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.2B, with a median of $1.8B. This represents modest appreciation from the current $1.6B valuation. Key uncertainties include capital intensity and commercialization timelines inherent to advanced nuclear technology, as well as the dispersion within the peer set—TerraPower trades at a significant premium, while earlier-stage Zap Energy sits at the lower bound. The range reflects reasonable sensitivity to technology de-risking and funding progress typical of late-stage frontier energy companies.
